Title: A secreted Ustilago maydis effector promotes virulence by targeting anthocyanin biosynthesis in maize

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: cytoplasmic serine/threonine protein kinase with C-terminal kinase domain and N-terminal phosphodegron-like DSGxS motif-containing region
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigmentation; UV-B protection; reactive oxygen species scavenging
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stabilized ZmTTK1 promotes anthocyanin induction in maize; in the absence of Tin2 it is destabilized via the ubiquitin-proteasome system
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in maize and N. benthamiana for functional assays; endogenous pattern not established
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast two-hybrid; GST pull-down/co-IP; transient expression in N. benthamiana and maize; MG132 treatment; phosphodegron mutagenesis; kinase assay; anthocyanin induction assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. maydis infection context; MG132 proteasome inhibition; co-expression with Tin2
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Stabilization favors anthocyanin accumulation but may reduce lignification and increase susceptibility to U. maydis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmTTK1 is a maize serine/threonine kinase identified as a Tin2 interactor. 2. Its phosphodegron-like motif promotes proteasomal degradation, while Tin2 binding stabilizes full-length active kinase. 3. Stabilized ZmTTK1 promotes anthocyanin induction and alters ZmR1 localization, linking pathogen effector action to host metabolic rewiring.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although it is a kinase regulator in pathway control, it is not a transcription factor; classified here as Common_Gene per instruction. Weak anthocyanin induction was observed with stabilized mutant ZmTTK1S279/282A alone in SG200Δtin2-infected leaves.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigmentation; UV-B protection; reactive oxygen species scavenging
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Serves as known positive anthocyanin regulator, but its localization is not noticeably altered by Tin2 and ZmTTK1 in this study
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient co-expression and confocal localization in maize epidermal cells
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Tin2; ZmTTK1 (tested functionally in co-expression context)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmC1 is included as a known positive regulator of maize anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. Unlike ZmR1, its nuclear localization is not significantly changed by co-expression with Tin2 and ZmTTK1. 3. This suggests Tin2-ZmTTK1 preferentially acts through ZmR1 rather than ZmC1 in the tested context.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally examined in Results, although the paper does not support it as the main mediator of Tin2 effects.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Early Golden Bantam; M541J (c2 mutant); M142V control
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form chalcone, the entry intermediate for flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigmentation; UV-B protection; reactive oxygen species scavenging
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool and commits flux toward anthocyanin/flavonoid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key entry/committed step for flavonoid biosynthesis; supported by c2 mutant phenotype discussed in paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly induced in SG200-infected maize leaves relative to SG200Δtin2-infected leaves at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue; anthocyanins accumulate in vacuole
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher ZmCHS expression is associated with Tin2-dependent anthocyanin accumulation; loss of CHS activity in c2 maize attenuates U. maydis virulence and abolishes anthocyanin induction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR of infected maize leaves; metabolite profiling; pathogen infection assay using maize c2 mutant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCHS transcript is upregulated in maize infected with wild-type U. maydis carrying tin2. 2. Tin2-dependent activation of the anthocyanin pathway correlates with strong anthocyanin accumulation. 3. In the maize c2 chalcone synthase mutant, anthocyanin induction is blocked and fungal virulence is reduced, supporting a causal role of CHS-mediated flux into flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ustilago maydis infection; comparison of SG200 vs SG200Δtin2; infected leaves collected at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Diverting precursor into anthocyanins may reduce lignin-related defenses
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix maize genome array, GEO GSE48536; metabolite profiling by UPLC-PDA-TOF-MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The c2 mutant experiment provided pathway-level evidence rather than direct transgenic manipulation of ZmCHS by the authors.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigmentation; UV-B protection; reactive oxygen species scavenging
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms early intermediate required for anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly induced in SG200-infected maize leaves relative to SG200Δtin2-infected leaves at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue; anthocyanins accumulate in vacuole
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tin2 presence correlates with increased ZmCHI expression and enhanced anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR of infected maize leaves; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCHI is among the five anthocyanin pathway genes examined by qRT-PCR. 2. Its transcript abundance is significantly higher in SG200-infected tissue than in SG200Δtin2-infected tissue. 3. This supports coordinated Tin2-dependent activation of the anthocyanin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. maydis infection; SG200 vs SG200Δtin2 at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix maize genome array, GEO GSE48536; metabolite profiling by UPLC-PDA-TOF-MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated as a core pathway gene because it was one of the experimentally quantified anthocyanin pathway genes in Results.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigmentation; UV-B protection; reactive oxygen species scavenging
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct intermediate for anthocyanin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly induced in SG200-infected maize leaves relative to SG200Δtin2-infected leaves at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue; anthocyanins accumulate in vacuole
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tin2-dependent induction of ZmF3H contributes to increased anthocyanin pathway flux
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR of infected maize leaves; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmF3H expression rises in response to infection by Tin2-producing U. maydis. 2. This induction is reduced/absent with the tin2 mutant. 3. The result supports broad activation of anthocyanin biosynthesis by Tin2.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. maydis infection; SG200 vs SG200Δtin2 at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix maize genome array, GEO GSE48536; metabolite profiling by UPLC-PDA-TOF-MS

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigmentation; UV-B protection; reactive oxygen species scavenging
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor of anthocyanidin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly induced in SG200-infected maize leaves relative to SG200Δtin2-infected leaves at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue; anthocyanins accumulate in vacuole
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tin2 promotes ZmDFR expression and thereby favors anthocyanin production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR of infected maize leaves; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmDFR is one of the five anthocyanin genes significantly induced in the presence of Tin2. 2. Reduced induction in SG200Δtin2 correlates with loss of cyanidin derivative accumulation. 3. The data support Tin2-driven enhancement of late anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. maydis infection; SG200 vs SG200Δtin2 at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix maize genome array, GEO GSE48536; metabolite profiling by UPLC-PDA-TOF-MS

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigmentation; UV-B protection; reactive oxygen species scavenging
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms anthocyanidin precursor to anthocyanin glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly induced in SG200-infected maize leaves relative to SG200Δtin2-infected leaves at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue; anthocyanins accumulate in vacuole
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tin2-dependent induction of ZmANS is associated with strong accumulation of cyanidin derivatives in infected maize leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR of infected maize leaves; UPLC-PDA-TOF-MS metabolite identification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmANS transcript is significantly induced in wild-type U. maydis-infected tissue. 2. This induction is diminished in tin2 mutant infections. 3. The expression pattern matches Tin2-dependent accumulation of cyanidin 3-glucoside and malonylated cyanidin derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. maydis infection; SG200 vs SG200Δtin2 at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix maize genome array, GEO GSE48536; metabolite profiling by UPLC-PDA-TOF-MS

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts indole-3-glycerol phosphate to indole, initiating benzoxazinoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense against aphids and fungal pathogens
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for DIBOA/DIMBOA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly different between SG200- and SG200Δtin2-infected maize leaves at 6 dpi; higher with SG200
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tin2 promotes expression of ZmBx1 and is associated with increased DIBOA-glucoside accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR of infected maize leaves; UPLC-PDA-TOF-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmBx1 is among DIMBOA-pathway genes differentially expressed depending on Tin2. 2. Tin2 presence correlates with higher transcript abundance and increased DIBOA-glucoside accumulation. 3. The study suggests Tin2 affects additional defense-metabolite pathways beyond anthocyanin.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. maydis infection; SG200 vs SG200Δtin2 at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix maize genome array, GEO GSE48536; metabolite profiling by UPLC-PDA-TOF-MS

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes indole in an early step of benzoxazinoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense against aphids and fungal pathogens
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms early intermediate for DIBOA/DIMBOA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly different between SG200- and SG200Δtin2-infected maize leaves at 6 dpi; higher with SG200
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tin2 increases ZmBx2 expression and correlates with elevated DIBOA-glucoside accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmBx2 is significantly affected by Tin2 during infection. 2. Increased expression in SG200-infected tissue matches the Tin2-dependent rise in DIBOA-glucoside. 3. Tin2 therefore influences both anthocyanin and benzoxazinoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. maydis infection; SG200 vs SG200Δtin2 at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix maize genome array, GEO GSE48536

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes monooxygenation in the conversion sequence leading to DIBOA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense against aphids and fungal pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly different between SG200- and SG200Δtin2-infected maize leaves at 6 dpi; higher with SG200
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tin2 promotes ZmBx5 expression and increases DIBOA-glucoside accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmBx5 expression is Tin2 responsive during maize infection. 2. The transcript change parallels changes in DIBOA-glucoside abundance. 3. This indicates Tin2 has broader effects on secondary metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. maydis infection; SG200 vs SG200Δtin2 at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix maize genome array, GEO GSE48536

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense against aphids and fungal pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly different between SG200- and SG200Δtin2-infected maize leaves at 6 dpi; higher with SG200
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tin2 promotes ZmBx8 expression and increases DIBOA-glucoside content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmBx8 is significantly upregulated in a Tin2-dependent manner. 2. Its function matches the measured increase in DIBOA-glucoside in SG200-infected leaves. 3. This supports a direct connection between Tin2 and benzoxazinoid output at least at the transcript/metabolite level.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. maydis infection; SG200 vs SG200Δtin2 at 6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix maize genome array, GEO GSE48536

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: smut effector; no conserved InterPro domain; C-terminal conserved region with PxxP motifs
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: secreted to apoplast, translocated to plant cytosol
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin biosynthesis activation; indirect modulation of lignin allocation; also affects DIMBOA/flavonoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein stabilization of host kinase ZmTTK1, leading to activation of anthocyanin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: induces expression of ZmCHS; ZmCHI; ZmF3H; ZmDFR; ZmANS; also increases ZmBx1; ZmBx2; ZmBx5; ZmBx8 transcript levels
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: expressed during biotrophic growth of U. maydis in maize
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential pathogen-derived inducer of anthocyanin accumulation in infected maize leaves
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigmentation; UV-B protection; reactive oxygen species scavenging
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tin2 promotes strong anthocyanin accumulation and enhances virulence; loss of tin2 abolishes anthocyanin induction and shifts host metabolism toward stronger lignification
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: induced during biotrophic growth, elevated during infection relative to axenic culture
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: biotrophic infection of maize; SG200 vs SG200Δtin2 comparisons
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for transient maize expression; otef and cmu1 promoters used in fungal constructs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion/complementation in U. maydis; transient expression in maize; secretion assays; yeast two-hybrid; in vitro pull-down; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tin2 is a secreted U. maydis effector induced during biotrophic growth and required for anthocyanin induction in infected maize. 2. Tin2 acts inside plant cells and interacts with/stabilizes the maize kinase ZmTTK1. 3. Tin2 presence causes transcriptional activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, accumulation of cyanidin derivatives, reduced lignification, and improved fungal virulence.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily a pathogen virulence factor rather than a crop improvement target; its host targets identify leverage points for engineering resistance by preventing Tin2 action or ZmTTK1 hijacking
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promotes pathogen virulence while increasing anthocyanin; can reduce lignin-associated defense and facilitate fungal spread
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix maize genome array, GEO GSE48536; metabolite profiling by UPLC-PDA-TOF-MS
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional C-terminus is essential; biologically inactive truncation Tin2(26-202) fails to bind/stabilize ZmTTK1 and fails to induce anthocyanin.

Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: cytoplasm and nucleus when expressed alone; predominantly nucleus when co-expressed with Tin2 and ZmTTK1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive transcriptional regulation; likely activated via Tin2-stabilized ZmTTK1-dependent phosphorylation and nuclear accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: anthocyanin biosynthetic genes including ZmCHS; ZmCHI; ZmF3H; ZmDFR; ZmANS
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Tin2 and ZmTTK1 co-expression enhances nuclear localization
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: known positive regulator required for anthocyanin biosynthesis in maize and implicated here as downstream transcriptional effector of Tin2-ZmTTK1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pigmentation; UV-B protection; reactive oxygen species scavenging
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZmR1 likely mediates Tin2/ZmTTK1-dependent induction of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes by accumulating in the nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient co-expression and confocal localization in maize epidermal cells
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmR1 is presented as a positive regulator of maize anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. Co-expression with Tin2 and ZmTTK1 shifts mCherry-ZmR1 to strong nuclear localization. 3. The data suggest ZmR1 acts downstream of Tin2-stabilized ZmTTK1 to activate anthocyanin gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ZmTTK1; Tin2 (functional link, not direct binding shown)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper suggests, but does not directly prove, that ZmTTK1 phosphorylates ZmR1.
